title: Choose the Right AI Model for Finance Work
description: Know exactly which AI model to use for each type of finance task — stop guessing and start matching the tool to the job.

steps.title: Understand the model landscape
steps.content: There are 4 main models worth knowing for finance: Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5.4, and Gemini 3 Flash. Each has a different sweet spot.

steps.title: Use Claude Sonnet 4.6 as your daily driver
steps.content: For 80% of daily finance work (quick analysis, formatting, drafting, data cleanup), Sonnet is fast, cheap, and scored #1 on the Finance Agent Benchmark. This is your default.

steps.title: Switch to Claude Opus 4.6 for complex reasoning
steps.content: When you need airtight logic — complex modeling, deep variance analysis, multi-step financial reasoning — Opus is the upgrade. It uses 5x fewer tokens than GPT-5 at similar accuracy.

steps.title: Use GPT-5.4 for heavy spreadsheet computation
steps.content: If you're building a massive Excel model and need pure number-crunching power, GPT-5.4 has the highest raw modeling score. Use it for computation-heavy tasks.

steps.title: Start teams on Gemini 3 Flash for budget-friendly entry
steps.content: At 83.6% accuracy and the lowest cost, Gemini 3 Flash is a solid option for teams just starting with AI. Good enough for daily work while keeping costs low.

steps.title: Never send client or regulator-facing work without human review
steps.content: No model has broken 64% on real-world analyst tasks (SEC filings, financial modeling, market research). Always have a human as the final check on anything external.

steps.title: Match your model to the task (cheat sheet)
steps.content:
- **Complex modeling** → Opus 4.6
- **Daily finance work** → Sonnet 4.6
- **Heavy computation** → GPT-5.4
- **Agentic workflows** → Sonnet 4.6
- **On a budget** → Gemini 3 Flash
- **Client/regulator-facing** → Human + any model
